heading: Benton Harbor Canal, Michigan
label: 53 Benton Harbor Canal, Michigan
text: The Benton Harbor Canal at and above the west line of Ninth Street, in the city of Benton Harbor and State of Michigan, is declared to be not a navigable water of the United States within the meaning of the Constitution and laws of the United States.The right to alter, amend, or repeal this section is expressly reserved.
notes: References in TextThis section, referred to in text, was in the original “this Act”, meaning , , sections 1 and 3 of which are classified to this section. Section 2 of the Act, which relates to abandonment of a portion of the Benton Harbor Canal project, is not classified to the Code.act June 2, 1937, ch. 28850 Stat. 243
